title: feat: MCPServer integration via Python SDK DaprMCPWorkflowClient
body:
## MCPServer Integration via Python SDK Client

dapr-agents consumes `DaprMCPWorkflowClient` from the Python SDK ([AI-536](https://linear.app/diagrid/issue/AI-536/feat-daprmcpworkflowclient-and-mcp-workflow-helpers)).

### Scope

* Remove old `MCPClient` from dapr-agents (replaced by SDK's `DaprMCPWorkflowClient`)
* Integrate SDK client into agent tool discovery: `DurableAgent` uses `DaprMCPWorkflowClient.get_all_tools()`
* Tools returned as `WorkflowContextInjectedTool` instances for agent use
* Quickstart example: DurableAgent + MCPServer CRD
* Dapr Agents docs: how to use MCPServer tools with agents
* Unit tests

### Related

* dapr/dapr#9731 (MCPServer workflow engine)
* [dapr/proposals#100](<https://github.com/dapr/proposals/issues/100>) (proposal - merged)

comments.body:
Hi @sicoyle, I noticed you have this self-assigned, but since it has the `help wanted` tag, I'd love to take this off your plate if you're not already working on it. 

I've been reviewing the proposal and the current `MCPClient` implementation. My plan for the `DaprMCPWorkflowClient` is to have `connect()` trigger the sidecar's `dapr.mcp.<name>.ListTools` orchestration to fetch and cache the schemas, and reuse `create_pydantic_model_from_schema` for the validation models. I'll then expose them as `WorkflowContextInjectedTool` instances where the inner executor just delegates execution to the sidecar via `ctx.call_child_workflow("dapr.mcp.<server_name>.CallTool", input={"tool": tool_name, "arguments": kwargs})`.

If this aligns with your vision, assign it over and I can get a draft PR up shortly.

> Hi @sicoyle, I noticed you have this self-assigned, but since it has the help wanted tag, I'd love to take this off your plate if you're not already working on it.

Hi!!! I appreciate your interest! I have the changes locally that I need to push so this is already done for the most part now :)
I will make more issues soon - it's on my todo list @Rishabh-git10 !!
